etymology.description: semantic 木(木) (mù, 'wood') + phonetic 羕(羕) (yàng) -> meaning
etymology.interpretation: The character 樣(樣) (yàng, 'appearance; shape; manner') combines the semantic component 木(木) (mù, 'wood') to suggest a tangible or physical form, with the phonetic 羕(羕) to provide the sound. The concept of 'appearance; shape; manner' relates to understanding form or style as if referring to forms made of or carved in wood.
etymology.simplification: 样(樣): 羊(羊) (yáng, 'sheep') replaced 羕(羕) (yàng) for a simpler phonetic component.

examples.chinese: 他的样子看起来很疲惫(他的樣子看起來很疲憊)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de yàngzi kàn qǐlái hěn pí bèi.

examples.chinese: 这件衣服的款式和样式很流行(這件衣服的款式和樣式很流行)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè jiàn yīfú de kuǎnshì hé yàngshì hěn liúxíng.
examples.english: The style and appearance of this clothing are very popular.

examples.chinese: 你应该学学他的工作样(你應該學學他的工作樣)。
examples.pinyin: Nǐ yīnggāi xué xué tā de gōngzuò yàng.
examples.english: You should learn his manner of working.
